vintage_claims_safe: Home Credit application_train has no absolute calendar timestamps — all date fields are relative integers (days/months before application)
SK_ID_CURR is sequential but DR is stable across quintiles (range=0.0033) — ID ordering does not proxy for temporal vintage drift
Age cohort analysis possible but reflects risk by demographics, not vintage drift
Monthly balance fields (MONTHS_BALANCE) in CC/POS tables are relative — cannot reconstruct absolute calendar
DAYS_DECISION in previous_application is relative to current application date — not absolute calendar

application_train.DAYS_BIRTH: relative_negative_int — days before application; not absolute date
application_train.DAYS_EMPLOYED: relative_negative_int — days employed before application; 365243=unemployed sentinel
application_train.DAYS_LAST_PHONE_CHANGE: relative_negative_int — days since last phone change
application_train.SK_ID_CURR: sequential integer application ID — potentially time-ordered if not shuffled; tested below

bureau.DAYS_CREDIT: relative — days before application when credit applied for
bureau.DAYS_CREDIT_ENDDATE: relative — days before application when credit ends
bureau.DAYS_CREDIT_UPDATE: relative — days before application of last update

previous_application.DAYS_DECISION: relative — days before application when decision made on prior app

credit_card_balance.MONTHS_BALANCE: relative negative integer — months before application

POS_CASH_balance.MONTHS_BALANCE: relative negative integer — months before application

recommended_production_monitoring: PSI monthly on top-20 SHAP features
Rolling 90-day actual DR on scored population
KS monthly on score distribution vs training distribution
proxy_cohort_safe_claim: Home Credit supports proxy-cohort analyses (by age, employment status, SK_ID_CURR quintile) but these reflect demographic risk patterns, not temporal vintage drift. True out-of-time validation is not possible with the public data release.
